implementationPlan.overview: Add `--headless` boolean CLI option to the `execute` command so it disables the textual UI and suppresses all stdout/stderr output (process returns exit code only). Minimal change: update execute() to accept flag, conditionally skip starting ui.render_ui, and guard any direct prints and exception messages. Optional: add a HeadlessWriter implementing UI for DI completeness.

pitfalls.avoidance: Detect pending_input/pending_permission and fail with a clear non-printed exit code; or implement pre-seeded inputs or auto-approve behavior if desired.
Do not raise click.ClickException in headless; instead call sys.exit with appropriate code. Audit prints across codebase and guard with headless flag.
pitfalls.issue: Click or Python printing errors to stderr despite headless

pitfalls.avoidance: If skipping ui_task, ensure nothing else awaits ui_task; main flow should await operation.execute_async and consider awaiting model.done or ensuring operation sets model.done correctly.
pitfalls.issue: Background UI tasks expecting to run may affect lifecycle

pitfalls.avoidance: Prefer conditional skipping of ui_task to avoid container changes. Only implement HeadlessWriter if further extensibility needed.

steps.changes: single-line addition of click.option decorator and function signature update.
steps.description: Modify src/lime_ai/app/agents/execute.py: add @click.option('--headless/--no-headless', default=False) and a headless parameter to execute(file_name, verify_prompts, allow_unverified, headless).

steps.changes: wrap ui_task creation in if not headless.
steps.description: In execute(), after creating operation and before starting UI, branch on headless: if not headless start ui_task = asyncio.create_task(ui.render_ui(model)); if headless, do not start ui_task.

steps.changes: wrap the print at line showing "File '...' does not exist." and any other print occurrences found across repo, or audit for prints and guard them.
steps.description: Replace direct prints and user-facing outputs with conditional checks `if not headless: print(...)` (e.g., file not found).

steps.changes: adjust exception handling block to check headless before raising click.ClickException; call sys.exit(1) when headless.
steps.description: Catch user-visible exceptions at top-level in execute(), and in headless mode exit silently with sys.exit(1) or raise SystemExit without message; ensure no error text is printed by re-raising a silent SystemExit.

steps.changes: small check after operation.execute_async or add helper to detect pending prompts; minimal change preferred: after execute_async completes, if model.pending_input or pending_permission and headless, sys.exit(2).
steps.description: Ensure ExecuteAgentOperation either fails fast or is monitored: after operation.execute_async returns or at defined checkpoints, detect execution_model.pending_input or pending_permission; if present and headless, set model.done=True and exit non-zero. Alternatively implement a HeadlessWriter that auto-denies/auto-fails pending prompts.

steps.changes: new class implementing UI.render_ui and optional container registration logic.
steps.description: Create HeadlessWriter in src/lime_ai/app/writers/writer.py or new file implementing UI with render_ui that blocks until model.done but does not display UI. Register in DI container when headless is used (update container wiring).

steps.changes: create tests under test/ if needed; manual validation steps described.
steps.description: Add or run tests validating headless mode: ensure no stdout/stderr for successful run, non-zero exit for missing file and for input-required mgx. Use existing test harness (pytest) or manual uv run commands.

steps.changes: small doc addition or one-line CLI help update.
steps.description: Update README.md or CLI help text to mention --headless behaviour and limitations (no interactive prompts or permissions).

implementationPlan.title: Implement --headless for execute command

request: I want to build a headless mode where the cli terminal ui is not shown.

edge_cases: Agent requests interactive input (pending_input) — in headless this will block; must decide behavior: fail with error, raise a specific exception, or supply inputs via API/config.
Permission prompts — headless cannot show overlays; decide default (deny, allow, or fail).
Plugins or code relying on UI side-effects (e.g., widgets that mutate state) — ensure skipping UI doesn't break plugin flows.
Exceptions and Click: click.ClickException and print() produce stderr/stdout; headless should avoid printing but still return non-zero exit code.
Long-running or background tasks started by UI code — ensure nothing depends on UI lifecycle to finish (ui_task awaiting).

files_to_modify.reason: Add --headless click option; when True do not start ui.render_ui task and avoid awaiting ui_task; suppress any prints and convert user-facing exceptions into silent exits.
Contains CliWriter implementing UI; consider adding HeadlessWriter class here or adapt existing writer to respect headless config (optional).
Contains LimeApp and UI rendering/poll loop; no change required if execute() simply does not start the UI, but if a HeadlessWriter is used, no changes to this file needed.
Defines UI interface; HeadlessWriter must implement this.
Dependency injection may need optional registration for HeadlessWriter when headless flag present (optional; alternative: skip UI task entirely).
Top-level click group; may not need change, but document new flag usage in help/README.
Logger outputs to file; check for print statements elsewhere that could leak stdout/stderr; ensure FileLogger is used for logs instead of printing to console.

functions_to_modify.reason: Add click.option('--headless/--no-headless') param, branch to skip ui task creation, and suppress user-facing prints/exceptions when headless.
Optional: make render_ui respect headless flag or create a HeadlessWriter.render_ui that returns immediately or awaits model.done without printing.

libraries_needed: No new third-party libraries; reuse existing dependencies (click, textual, rich).
new_functions: HeadlessWriter.render_ui(execution_model) -> implementing UI that emits no output (could simply await model.done and return).
helper in execute: _exit_silently(code:int) -> perform silent exit without printing (careful with Click semantics).
researchResults.notes: Minimal code changes recommended: change only src/lime_ai/app/agents/execute.py to add the flag and conditionally skip ui_task, plus small guards around print() and error handling. Optional enhancements: HeadlessWriter and container registration for cleaner DI and future expansion.

potential_pitfalls_and_mitigation.mitigation: Detect execution_model.pending_input or pending_permission and fail early with a clear non-printed exit code or provide an API to pre-seed inputs/auto-approve.
potential_pitfalls_and_mitigation.pitfall: ExecutionModel awaiting input/permission will hang with no UI.

potential_pitfalls_and_mitigation.mitigation: In headless mode convert user-facing exceptions into SystemExit with no message or handle exceptions and call sys.exit(code) after suppressing output (test to ensure click runner doesn't print).
potential_pitfalls_and_mitigation.pitfall: Click will display exception messages to stderr even if UI is suppressed.

potential_pitfalls_and_mitigation.mitigation: Search and audit code for print() calls (e.g., file existence message in execute) and wrap them to check headless flag before printing.
potential_pitfalls_and_mitigation.pitfall: Other code prints directly to stdout (debug prints) bypassing writers.

potential_pitfalls_and_mitigation.mitigation: Prefer skipping ui_task in execute to avoid DI changes; alternatively register HeadlessWriter into container during startup when headless enabled.
potential_pitfalls_and_mitigation.pitfall: DI container returns CliWriter by default; switching to HeadlessWriter may require container changes.

recommended_implementation_steps.step: Add option to execute(): @click.option('--headless/--no-headless', default=False) and accept headless param.
In execute(), after creating execution operation and before creating ui_task, branch: if headless: do not create ui_task and do not await it; else create ui_task as current behavior.

Suppress any direct prints when headless (e.g., file-not-found message): replace 'print(...)' with conditional: if not headless: print(...). For prompt/exception handling, in headless catch exceptions and call sys.exit(1) without printing.
(Optional) Implement HeadlessWriter class implementing UI.render_ui that awaits model.done (poll or await event) and returns; register it in container when headless is active if DI approach chosen.

Add unit/integration tests: run execute with --headless on a simple .mgx that completes normally, ensure no stdout/stderr, and exit code 0; test .mgx that requires input to ensure headless returns non-zero and does not hang.
Update README or CLI help to document --headless behavior, and mention that interactive prompts/permission requests are unsupported in headless mode.

researchResults.summary: Implement a --headless flag for the 'execute' command that disables the textual UI and suppresses all stdout/stderr output (only exit code returned). Two viable approaches: (A) conditionally skip starting the UI task in execute() and ensure no prints/exceptions produce output; (B) register a Headless UI implementation of UI that emits no output and ensures input/permission requests fail fast or are auto-handled. Recommend approach (A) + optional Headless UI for cleaner DI.

testing_and_validation.test: Run 'uv run python src/main.py execute sample.mgx --headless' on known-good mgx; assert no stdout/stderr and exit code 0.
Run execute with nonexistent file and --headless; assert no stdout/stderr and non-zero exit code.
Run an mgx that requests input and --headless; assert it exits non-zero rather than hanging.
